coin_profile.name: Cardano
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Cardano is a proof-of-stake Layer 1 blockchain built on peer-reviewed academic research. It uses the Ouroboros consensus mechanism and is developed by IOHK. Cardano focuses on scalability, sustainability, and interoperability, with significant adoption in developing-world identity and financial inclusion projects.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: ADA has moderate moat strength anchored by 8-year protocol maturity, reasonable decentralization, and established staking infrastructure, but is undermined by weak real-world utility (low DeFi TVL relative to peers), limited ecosystem lock-in compared to ETH/SOL, and founder-driven governance that creates regulatory/centralization perception risks. The protocol is forkable and has faced competitive pressure from faster L1s; while Cardano's academic rigor and Ouroboros consensus provide some defensibility, the network demand signals (4/10) and capital efficiency (3/10) indicate the moat has not translated into dominant market position or sustainable value capture for token holders.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.

coin_profile.description: Avalanche is a high-speed, low-cost Layer 1 platform known for its subnet architecture, which allows anyone to launch custom blockchains. It achieves sub-second finality and hosts a growing DeFi ecosystem. AVAX is used for network fees, staking, and governance across the Avalanche platform.

moat.rationale: AVAX has moderate competitive defensibility with a functional L1 but lacks the settlement-layer dominance of ETH or security track record of BTC; it has been forked and remains vulnerable to faster/cheaper L1s. Network effects are present but weakening as users migrate to cheaper alternatives (Solana, Arbitrum), and while the token captures validator rewards and gas fees, holder value accrual (10/10 signal) is undermined by dilution concerns (4/10 dilution control) and declining network demand (4/10). Ecosystem lock-in exists via Avalanche subnets and developer tooling but lacks the irreversibility of Bitcoin or Ethereum's settlement dominance, positioning AVAX as a capable but non-essential L1 in a crowded market.
